name: Charlie Parker
description:
'''Charles Parker Jr.''' (August 29, 1920 – March 12, 1955), nicknamed "'''Bird'''" or "'''Yardbird'''", was an American [[jazz]] [[Saxophone|saxophonist]], bandleader, and composer. Parker was a highly influential soloist and leading figure in the development of [[bebop]], a form of jazz characterized by fast tempos, [[Virtuoso|virtuosic]] technique, and advanced harmonies. He was a virtuoso and introduced revolutionary rhythmic and harmonic ideas into jazz, including rapid [[passing chord]]s, new variants of [[altered chord]]s, and [[chord substitution]]s. Parker primarily played the [[alto saxophone]].

Parker was an icon for the [[hipster (1940s subculture)|hipster]] subculture and later the [[Beat Generation]], personifying the jazz musician as an uncompromising artist and intellectual rather than just an entertainer.
